differences.title: 1. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Compiles to binary code for fast execution, ideal for heavy computations.
differences.rightDesc: Interpreted at runtime, generally slower for CPU-intensive tasks but highly versatile.

differences.title: 2. LANGUAGE SUPPORT
differences.leftDesc: Can be compiled from C, C++, Rust, Go, and more.
differences.rightDesc: Primarily JavaScript, though transpiled languages exist.

differences.title: 3. MEMORY MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: Provides fine-grained control over memory for optimal performance.
differences.rightDesc: Automatic memory management through a garbage collector.

differences.title: 4. DOM ACCESS
differences.leftDesc: Requires JavaScript glue code to interact with the Document Object Model.
differences.rightDesc: Directly manipulates the DOM, enabling dynamic web interfaces.

differences.title: 5. SECURITY
differences.leftDesc: Runs in a secure sandbox, preventing direct access to host system resources.
differences.rightDesc: Can be susceptible to XSS and other browser-based security flaws.

differences.title: 6. ECOSYSTEM
differences.leftDesc: Still evolving, focused on performance-critical libraries and tools.
differences.rightDesc: Enormous ecosystem of libraries, frameworks, and tools for all web tasks.

differences.title: 7. USE CASES
differences.leftDesc: Games, video editing, CAD, scientific simulations, and computationally heavy apps.
differences.rightDesc: UI development, API interactions, dynamic content, and standard web applications.
